title: microlepia hirsuta
intro: Microlepia hirsuta, also known as the hairy spleenwort, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used in Asian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Microlepia hirsuta include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and topical applications, although its use may vary depending on the cultural and traditional practices.
